items.headline: Election called for November 3, 2026, for three council seats
items.summary: Los Gatos will hold a general municipal election on November 3, 2026, to fill three City Council positions. Residents can expect candidate filings and campaigning over the summer and fall.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: The City Council approved the final subdivision map for Tract 10675 on Boulevard, clearing a project for development. Without access to the full project details, residents in or near that area should check the city's development tracker for specifics on what will be built.

items.headline: Council confirms 2026 weed abatement assessment report
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution confirming the 2026 weed abatement assessment report, which authorizes the city to assess property owners for weed removal on their lots.

items.headline: FY 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant funding discussion
items.summary: City Council discussed allocation of federal Community Development Block Grant funds for FY 2026-27. These grants support housing, infrastructure, and social services in Cupertino.

items.headline: Four downtown digital signs receive permit modifications
items.summary: Matrix sign graphics at 250 Hamilton, 445 Bryant, 520 Webster, and 528 High Street have been cleared for design changes under existing planning approvals. These are downtown commercial corridor locations where digital signage updates may affect the streetscape.

items.headline: Council to review 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on May 27 to review the proposed 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan and its compliance with the Comprehensive Plan, with a recommendation to the Finance Committee.

items.headline: Single-family home addition with rear setback variance requested
items.summary: A homeowner is seeking design review and zoning variance to add 730 square feet to a one-story house, reducing the rear yard setback from the required 10 feet to 7 feet 4 inches. The addition would increase the home to 2,592 square feet and bring rear yard encroachment to 34 percent, exceeding the 25 percent maximum.

items.headline: Tech facility receives permit for specialized gas equipment
items.summary: A $600,000 permit was issued for installation of a CLF3 gas cabinet at 3011 N 1st St, suggesting semiconductor or advanced manufacturing work in San José's industrial corridor. The scale of investment indicates a significant tenant or expansion in the tech/manufacturing sector.
